original_abstract: Abstract Two- or one-electron-mediated difunctionalizations of internal alkenes represent straightforward approaches to assemble molecular complexity by the simultaneous formation of two contiguous C sp 3 stereocentres. Although racemic versions have been extensively explored, asymmetric variants, especially those involving open-shell C-centred radical species, are much more limited both in number and scope. Here we describe enantioenriched arylsulfinylamides as all-in-one reagents for the efficient asymmetric, intermolecular aminoarylation of alkenes. Under mild photoredox conditions, nitrogen addition of the arylsulfinylamide onto the double bond, followed by 1,4-translocation of the aromatic ring, produce, in a single operation, the corresponding aminoarylation adducts in enantiomerically enriched form. The sulfinyl group acts here as a traceless chiral auxiliary, as it is eliminated in situ under the mild reaction conditions. Optically pure β,β-diarylethylamines, aryl-α,β-ethylenediamines and α-aryl-β-aminoalcohols, prominent motifs in pharmaceuticals, bioactive natural products and ligands for transition metals, are thereby accessible with excellent levels of regio-, relative and absolute stereocontrol.
humanized_abstract: Two- or one-electron-mediated difunctionalizations of internal alkenes to produce two C sp 3 stereocentres in a single operation have emerged as popular strategies in constructing complex molecules. While racemic variants of such reactions have been studied in great detail, their chiral versions, especially those involving open-shell C-centred radical species, remain scarce and often restricted to narrow ranges of applicability. In this communication, we show for the first time that enantioenriched arylsulfinylamides can serve as all-in-one reagents to access the asymmetric intermolecular aminoarylation of alkenes under mild photoredox conditions. In this mode, the nitrogen addition of the arylsulfinylamide to the alkene is coupled with a 1,4-translocation of the aromatic ring to give, in one-pot operation, aminoarylation products in high enantioselectivities. In these reactions the sulfinyl group serves as a simple traceless chiral auxiliary that is activated and simultaneously removed during the course of the mild reaction conditions applied. Under these conditions diverse β,β-diarylethylamines, aryl-α,β-ethylenediamines and α-aryl-β-aminoalcohols can be synthesized with high levels of regioselectivity, absolute and relative stereocontrol, all key structural features that are represented in numerous bioactive natural products and/or pharmaceuticals as well as in commonly used transition metal ligands.

In this communication, we show for the first time that enantioenriched arylsulfinylamides can serve as all-in-one reagents to access the asymmetric intermolecular aminoarylation of alkenes under mild photoredox conditions. In this mode, the nitrogen addition of the arylsulfinylamide to the alkene is coupled with a 1,4-translocation of the aromatic ring to give, in one-pot operation, aminoarylation products in high enantioselectivities. Under these conditions diverse β,β-diarylethylamines, aryl-α,β-ethylenediamines and α-aryl-β-aminoalcohols can be synthesized with high levels of regioselectivity, absolute and relative stereocontrol, all key structural features that are represented in numerous bioactive natural products and/or pharmaceuticals as well as in commonly used transition metal ligands.
